texto: Americans see democracy as a remedy for all ills-to be taken three times daily like prescription medicine. It works for them. Ergo - it should work for the world. What America naïvely forgets is that for democracy to function, most of a populace must have something personally that is worth preserving.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Arthur Hailey: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Americans see democracy as a remedy for all ills-to be taken three times daily like prescription …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, america, americans pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e americans.

What America naïvely forgets is that for democracy to function, most of a populace must have something personally that is worth preserving.

texto: Don't put down too many roots in terms of a domicile. I have lived in four countries and I think my life as a writer and our family's life have been enriched by this. I think a writer has to experience new environments. There is that adage: No man can really succeed if he doesn't move away from where he was born. I believe it is particularly true for the writer.
explicacao: Citação de Arthur Hailey sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Don't put down too many roots in terms of a domicile. I have lived in four countries and I think …” articula vida, countries, domicile em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como domicile e experiences.

texto: I have been extraordinarily lucky. Anyone who pretends that some kind of luck isn't involved in his success is deluding himself.
explicacao: Citação de Arthur Hailey sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “I have been extraordinarily lucky.
